pull.title: validation: compact chainstate after connecting assumevalid block
pull.body:
**Problem:** [bitcoin-core/leveldb-subtree#61](https://github.com/bitcoin-core/leveldb-subtree/pull/61) disabled read-triggered seek compactions to avoid large chainstate write amplification from random UTXO lookups. That avoids repeated read-driven rewrites, but it can also leave more data in upper LevelDB levels and increase chainstate disk usage after initial sync.

**Fix:** Compact the chainstate once after connecting the configured assumevalid block. The default assumevalid block is updated with major releases, so this gives initial sync a release-tracking cleanup point without tying the trigger to the process-local IBD latch or adding recurring compaction scheduling. The chainstate is force-flushed before queueing compaction so LevelDB compacts the latest connected state.

verification.payload:

committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1780587767 +0200

coins: test chainstate flush baseline

Expose a narrow LevelDB property pass-through for chainstate tests and use it to assert existing flush behavior before adding manual chainstate compaction.

After flushing one coin and best-block hash, the values are readable while `leveldb.num-files-at-level2` still reports no table files.

author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1780587137 +0200
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1780588787 +0200

db: extract wrapper compaction method

Expose `CDBWrapper::CompactFull()` so callers can request the same full LevelDB compaction already used by `-forcecompactdb` without reaching into wrapper internals.
Extend the coins DB flush baseline to reopen the same DB with `force_compact` and check that LevelDB reports a level-2 table file while the flushed coin and best block remain readable.

coins: add chainstate compaction method

Add a CCoinsViewDB wrapper for blocking full LevelDB compaction so validation code can compact the chainstate without reaching through to the lower-level DB wrapper.
Extend the existing coins DB flush baseline to check that compaction produces LevelDB files at the expected level while preserving the flushed coin and best block.

author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1779999463 +0200
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1780588787 +0200

validation: compact chainstate at assumevalid

A node builds most of its chainstate before connecting the release's configured assumevalid block and then leaves the resulting LevelDB layout to ordinary background compaction.
Queue a full chainstate compaction when the connected tip equals `AssumedValidBlock()` instead of tying the trigger to the process-local IBD latch or adding recurring scheduling.

The compaction is queued after a force flush so connected work up to the assumevalid block is visible to LevelDB, and skipped if shutdown is already requested before the callback runs.
The functional test starts a second node with `-assumevalid` set to a mined block, checks compaction logs at that block, and verifies that a stale restart can leave IBD again without compacting once the assumevalid block was already connected.

Co-authored-by: Andrew Toth <andrewstoth@gmail.com>

* Possibly due to a silent merge conflict (the changes in this pull request being
incompatible with the current code in the target branch). If so, make sure to rebase on the latest
commit of the target branch.

* A sanitizer issue, which can only be found by compiling with the sanitizer and running the

* An intermittent issue.

Leave a comment here, if you need help tracking down a confusing failure.

comments.body: Could we retrieve the assumed valid block from `m_chainman.GetConsensus().defaultAssumevalid` instead?
